scenario_name: Scenario 11 — Queue Backpressure and Consumer Lag
justification: All pass criteria met: throughput gap clearly identified (300/min vs 15-20/min with ~280/min deficit), unbounded queue growth described with specific projections (16,800 backlog in 1 hour), visibility timeout double-processing risk identified with precise math (10×3s≈30s=timeout), missing DLQ identified as a blocker, queue depth metric gap called out as critical, autoscaling recommended (minimum 15-20 instances), verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptional throughput math — calculated deficit, time-to-backlog milestones, and framed it as 15-20× underprovisioned
Visibility timeout analysis goes beyond identification — explains it happens under NORMAL operation, not just edge cases, and provides three concrete fix options
DLQ absence framed correctly as a blocker with clear reasoning about poison messages consuming capacity
Queue depth metric gap called out as THE leading indicator and single most important missing metric
Escalations section correctly routes capacity gap to architecture and duplicate processing to domain correctness
Goes beyond rubric requirements with idempotency analysis, per-message deletion recommendation, and health check critique
Confidence assessment honestly flags unknowns (burst vs sustained, image size distribution) without hedging on the clear findings
